search_queries_used: site:nice.org.uk NG98 persistent otalgia discharge hearing referral
site:entnet.org acute otitis externa pain modifiers follow-up
site:nice.org.uk NG91 ear pain reassessment children
candidate_sources_rejected: single ear-infection guideline applied to every otalgia follow-up
automatic antibiotic, eardrop, analgesic, imaging, dental, TMJ, or referral plan
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Ear pain may be canal, middle-ear, referred, traumatic, postoperative, neurological, or malignant.
No diagnosis, investigation, medicine, dose, treatment, referral, or urgency is generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with ongoing ear pain receive partial NG98 support; children and cause-specific canal or middle-ear disease require separate age- and diagnosis-specific evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary, urgent, dental, audiology, or ENT follow-up.
UAE_applicability: International guidance requires UAE paediatric, antimicrobial, dental, audiology, imaging, ENT, and referral adaptation.
recency_verification: Current NICE NG98, NICE NG91, and AAO-HNSF acute otitis externa exact sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: NG98 and NG91 remain current; the 2014 AAO-HNSF update remains listed with material recency uncertainty.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact adult hearing section supports interval pain, discharge, hearing, tinnitus, vertigo, asymmetry, relevant negatives, ENT examination, and clinician concern without determining the source of otalgia.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports unilateral or asymmetric loss, tinnitus, vertigo, persistent ear pain or discharge, abnormal outer-ear or tympanic-membrane findings, wax, and selected MRI referral contexts.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact audiology section supports concerns, reviewed hearing information, entered plan, safety net, follow-up, and questions without recommending medicine, imaging, or referral.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires full relevant history, hearing and communication needs, psychosocial and activity impact, otoscopy, pure-tone audiometry, tympanometry when indicated, personalised plans, hearing options, follow-up, questions, and support.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact acute-otitis-externa symptom section was reviewed for canal pain and modifying factors, but no additional broad legacy text was mapped because follow-up otalgia can have non-canal causes.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports swimmer’s-ear terminology, water exposure, ear-canal inflammation, itching, fullness, hearing change, external-ear pain or tenderness, diabetes, immunocompromise, and tympanic-membrane context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact paediatric acute-otitis-media section was reviewed for age-limited reassessment context, but no adult or cause-agnostic text was mapped.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines the pediatric acute-otitis-media population, usual duration, reassessment triggers, otorrhoea context, and serious-illness review.

unresolved_source_gaps: Age, laterality, pain character, fever, discharge, hearing, vertigo, jaw or dental features, examination, diagnosis, treatment, response, investigation, referral, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
